commit.message:
Improve AI recognition accuracy

Shape recognition:
- Hybrid geometric + Protractor approach with corner detection, circularity,
  RDP simplification, and convex hull analysis
- Arrow detection runs before closed-shape analysis to prevent arrow→triangle
  misclassification
- Enhanced arrow detector with shaft+head split at multiple fractions and
  elongation-based fallback
- Diamond detection moved before triangle to prevent overlap
- Closeness-based closed/open classification instead of binary threshold

Handwriting recognition:
- Disabled Tesseract dictionary (load_system_dawg=0, load_freq_dawg=0) to
  prevent character substitution (e.g. 'youssef' → 'you say')
- Run TrOCR and Tesseract in parallel, pick highest confidence result
- Increased canvas resolution (300px height for Tesseract, 128px for TrOCR)
- Thicker strokes and more padding for clearer OCR input
- Multiple PSM modes (7, 8, 6, 13) tried in sequence for best result
- TrOCR runs in dedicated Web Worker (non-blocking)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix race condition in annex-event-replay.ts clearForAgent (#685)

Fixes the race condition in `clearForAgent` where iterating the buffer
array in reverse with `splice` could interleave with `pushEvent` calls
from different event handlers via microtasks, leading to missed events
or index errors during replay.

## Changes Made

Replaced the in-place reverse `splice` loop in `clearForAgent` with an
atomic filter-and-replace approach:
- Use `buffer.filter()` to create a new array excluding the target
agent's events
- Atomically replace the buffer contents using `buffer.length = 0`
followed by a loop to push back the filtered events
- Avoids spreading the filtered array to prevent potential stack
overflow with large buffers (up to 10,000 events)

## Testing

- All 11 existing tests pass
- No security issues found (CodeQL clean)

commit.message:
Fix inconsistent any types in IPC handlers (#698)

Replaces `any` types with proper shared types at the IPC boundary where
type safety matters most.

## Changes Made

- **`src/main/ipc/agent-handlers.ts:78`**: Replaced `updates: any` with
`DurableConfigUpdates`, a local type alias derived from the
`updateDurableConfig` function signature using `Parameters<typeof
agentConfig.updateDurableConfig>[2]`
- **`src/main/ipc/agent-handlers.ts:248`**: Replaced `opts: any` with
`StructuredSessionOpts` imported from `../orchestrators/types`
- **`src/main/ipc/plugin-handlers.ts:101`**: Replaced `manifest: any`
with `PluginManifest` imported from `../../shared/plugin-types`
- **`src/preload/index.ts:149`**: Replaced `hookEvent: any` with
`AgentHookEvent` imported from `../shared/types`

## Testing

- ✅ TypeScript compilation passes (`tsc --noEmit`)
- ✅ All 1944 tests pass across 83 test files
- ✅ No CodeQL security alerts

> ## Description
> Multiple handlers use `any` for parameters that have well-defined
types in the shared module. Bypasses TypeScript at the IPC boundary

> ## Impact
> Type errors at the IPC boundary are not caught at compile time,
leading to potential runtime errors that are harder to debug.
> 
> ## Fix Suggestion

Fix body reading inconsistency in /pair endpoint (#705)

Fixes the `/pair` endpoint body reading inconsistency (CQ-25). The
`/pair` endpoint was reading the request body inline without size
limits, while all other POST endpoints used the `readBody()` helper.
This made `/pair` vulnerable to memory exhaustion from arbitrarily large
request bodies.

## Changes Made

- **Size limit**: Added a 1MB size limit (`MAX_BODY_SIZE`) to
`readBody()`, checking `body.length + chunk.length` before concatenation
to strictly enforce the limit and protect against memory exhaustion
across all endpoints.
- **Consistent body reading**: Refactored `/pair` to use `readBody()`
and `parseJsonBody()` matching the pattern used by all other POST
endpoints.
- **Type validation**: Added explicit type validation for the `pin`
field to ensure it's a string before comparison.
- **Error handling**: Added proper `.catch()` error handling with
`appLog` for readBody failures on `/pair`, matching the error handling
pattern of other endpoints.

## Testing

- ✅ All 40 existing annex-server tests pass
- ✅ No CodeQL security alerts

commit.message:
consensus: Fix fork resolution deadlock with 3 interconnected bugs

Bug 1: check_2_3_consensus_for_production() longest-chain escape allowed
solo block production during same-height forks. Now detects when peers at
our height have different hashes and blocks production until resolved.

Bug 2: Same-height fork resolution required >50% weighted majority, which
is impossible when N nodes are on N different chains. Now uses deterministic
hash tiebreaker (lower hash wins) directly for same-height forks.

Bug 3: Non-consensus peer blocking prevented behind nodes from syncing when
consensus_peers was stale or wrong. Now allows syncing from any peer when
gap exceeds 20 blocks to break deadlock.

commit.message:
Fix refCount race condition in config pipeline (#684)

Fixes the refCount race condition in `restoreForAgent` where two agents
exiting concurrently can decrement `refCount` twice before either checks
`<= 0`, causing `restoreSnapshot` to be called twice and potentially
corrupting configuration state.

## Changes Made

- **Atomic decrement-and-check in `restoreForAgent`**: Moved
`snapshots.delete(absPath)` before `restoreSnapshot()` so that once
refCount hits zero, the snapshot is immediately removed from the map,
preventing any concurrent caller from finding and double-restoring it.
- **Early agent tracking removal**: Moved `agentFiles.delete(agentId)`
before the file iteration loop so that a second concurrent call with the
same agentId returns early as a no-op.
- **New tests**: Added two test cases covering the double-call scenario
(same agent calling `restoreForAgent` twice) and concurrent agent
cleanup not double-restoring the same file.

## Testing

- ✅ All 28 config-pipeline tests pass
- ✅ CodeQL security scan reports no alerts

commit.message:
Fix unnecessary store instances in notificationStore (#682)

Fixes a memory leak where `isAgentVisible()` in `notificationStore.ts`
called `getProjectHubStore(projectId)`, which creates a new hub store if
one doesn't exist. Every notification check for a new project would
create and cache an unnecessary hub store instance.

## Changes Made

- **`src/renderer/plugins/builtin/hub/main.ts`**: Added
`hasProjectHubStore(projectId)` — a read-only lookup that checks if a
hub store already exists in the `projectHubStores` map without creating
one.
- **`src/renderer/stores/notificationStore.ts`**: Guarded the
`getProjectHubStore()` call in `isAgentVisible()` with
`hasProjectHubStore()`, so the store is only accessed if it already
exists. If no store exists for that project, the hub pane visibility
check is skipped (since no hub store means the agent can't be visible in
a hub pane).
- **`src/renderer/plugins/builtin/hub/main.test.ts`**: Added 4 tests for
`hasProjectHubStore()` covering: export exists, returns false for
unknown projects, returns false for null, and returns true after store
creation.

## Testing

- All 39 tests pass (35 existing + 4 new)
- Code review: clean

commit.message:
fix: remove ZEN2/ZEN3/SKYLAKE from OpenBLAS DYNAMIC_LIST

OpenBLAS 0.3.31 compiles generic C dot/nrm2 kernels for ZEN2, ZEN3 and
SKYLAKE using AVX2+FMA intrinsics but omits -mfma from the compiler
invocation when DYNAMIC_LIST is set, causing an always_inline target
mismatch build error on all platforms.

Fix: remove the three offending targets:
- Generic list: NEHALEM SANDYBRIDGE HASWELL SKYLAKEX ZEN (was +ZEN2 ZEN3)
- AVX2 list:    HASWELL SKYLAKEX (was +SKYLAKE ZEN2 ZEN3)

ZEN2/ZEN3 users fall back to ZEN kernel (still AVX2); Skylake users fall
back to HASWELL — negligible performance difference in practice.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: rename Alumni.svg to alumni.svg and deploy testing on merge (#93)

## Problem

Two issues were causing the mobile app to misbehave on the testing
environment:

### 1. Missing `alumni.svg` (case sensitivity)
The asset file was `assets/images/Alumni.svg` (capital A) on macOS
(case-insensitive filesystem), but the Flutter-generated code and nginx
on the Linux Docker container expected `assets/images/alumni.svg`
(lowercase). This caused a 404 on every page that renders the alumni
logo.

### 2. Testing environment not updated on PR merge
The `deploy-testing` job only ran on pull request events, not on push to
`main`. So when PR #91 merged, only **production** was updated — the
testing environment kept running the old code.

## Changes
- `assets/images/Alumni.svg` → `assets/images/alumni.svg` (rename, Linux
case fix)
- `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` — `deploy-testing` now also runs on
`push` to `main`

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
